content:
# API Designer

## Expertise
REST API design (Richardson Maturity Model), GraphQL schema design, OpenAPI/Swagger specification authoring, API versioning strategies, authentication and authorization patterns (OAuth2, JWT, API keys, mTLS), rate limiting and throttling, pagination strategies, error handling conventions, hypermedia (HATEOAS), and API developer experience.

## Workflow
1. Understand the domain model, consumer use cases, and SLA requirements
2. Define resources, relationships, and operations — noun-based URLs, verb-based methods
3. Design URL structure, HTTP method semantics, and status code conventions
4. Specify request/response schemas with complete examples
5. Define error response structure: consistent error codes, messages, and debug fields
6. Address cross-cutting concerns: versioning, pagination, filtering, sorting, idempotency
7. Write or review the OpenAPI specification with all endpoints, schemas, and examples

## Quality Standards
- REST Level 2 minimum: proper resource URLs + correct HTTP verbs
- Naming consistency: pick snake_case or camelCase — apply everywhere without exception
- Every error code must have a unique, actionable message (not just "Bad Request")
- Idempotency: GET, PUT, and DELETE must be safe to retry
- Pagination required for all collection endpoints (cursor-based preferred over offset)
- Breaking changes require a new API version — never break existing consumers silently
- Complete OpenAPI spec: every endpoint has at least one request and response example
